questions.question: Which of the following is a prose fiction by Chinua Achebe?
A soliloquy is a speech delivered:
options: By a character alone on stage to himself/herself

questions.question: The term 'protagonist' refers to:
Which literary device involves giving human qualities to non-human things?
A haiku is a form of poetry originating from:
The climax of a story is:
options: The turning point or moment of highest tension

questions.question: Which of the following best describes an epic?
options: A long narrative poem about heroic deeds

questions.question: Irony occurs when:
options: What is said is exactly what is meant
What is said is the opposite of what is meant
A character speaks directly to the audience
Two characters have the same name

questions.question: The setting of a story refers to:
options: The time and place where the story occurs

questions.question: A tragedy is a play that ends in:
options: The downfall or death of the protagonist

questions.question: Alliteration is the repetition of:
options: The same consonant sound at the beginning of words
The same word at the end of lines

questions.question: The omniscient narrator:
options: Is a character in the story
Knows everything about all characters and events
Only knows what one character thinks

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of a sonnet?
options: 14 lines with a specific rhyme scheme

questions.question: Catharsis in drama refers to:
options: The emotional purging experienced by the audience
Comic relief in a serious play

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a simile?
options: The sun is a golden coin
The angry sea devoured the ship

questions.question: The conflict in a story refers to:
Which literary period is associated with William Shakespeare?
An aside in drama is:
options: A conversation between two main characters
Words spoken by a character that other characters on stage cannot hear
A lengthy speech by the narrator
The final scene of a play

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a genre of literature?
The theme of a literary work is:
options: The central message or idea explored in the work

questions.question: Onomatopoeia refers to words that:
The denouement of a play is:
options: The final resolution or untangling of the plot

questions.question: A flat character is one who:
options: Undergoes significant change throughout the story
Has only one or two traits and does not develop
Is the main character of the story

questions.question: Which of the following best describes free verse?
options: Poetry that follows a strict rhyme scheme
Poetry with a fixed number of syllables per line
Poetry that does not follow regular rhyme or meter

questions.question: The antagonist in a story is:
options: The character who opposes the protagonist

questions.question: Foreshadowing is a literary device used to:
options: Hint at future events in the story

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of oral literature?
options: It is transmitted verbally from generation to generation
It has a single known author

questions.question: A round character is one who:
options: Is a minor character with no significant role
Is complex, multi-dimensional and undergoes change
Always appears at the beginning of the story

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of hyperbole?
options: The wind whispered through the trees
She is as brave as a lion
I have told you a million times
The night was a dark blanket

questions.question: A flashback is a narrative technique that:
options: Returns to earlier events in the story

questions.question: The exposition of a story provides:
options: Background information about characters and setting

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a ballad?
options: It is a long philosophical poem
It is a narrative poem, often with a refrain, about love or adventure
It is always written in free verse

questions.question: Dramatic irony occurs when:
options: The audience knows something a character does not
A character says the opposite of what they mean

questions.question: The term 'diction' in literature refers to:
options: The plot structure of a story

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of African oral literature?
A metaphor directly compares two things:
Which of the following is a feature of tragedy according to Aristotle?
options: A hamartia (tragic flaw) in the protagonist

questions.question: The narrative technique of stream of consciousness attempts to:
options: Tell a story in strict chronological order
Depict the continuous flow of thoughts in a character's mind

questions.question: Which of the following best describes satire?
options: The use of humour, irony, or exaggeration to criticise human vices or follies
A romantic story with a happy ending

questions.question: The foil character in a story:
options: Contrasts with another character to highlight their qualities
Is the main character's best friend
Appears only at the end of the story

questions.question: An ode is a type of poem that:
options: Is a lyric poem of praise or celebration

questions.question: Which of the following best defines plot?
options: The time and place of the story
The sequence of events in a story
The central theme of the work

questions.question: In poetry, a caesura is:
options: The last line of a stanza
A pause within a line of poetry
The rhythm pattern of the poem

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of verbal irony?
options: An audience knows a bomb is under a table but characters do not
A character says 'What lovely weather!' during a storm
A character's flaw leads to their downfall
A story ends differently from expectations

questions.question: The term 'bildungsroman' refers to a novel about:
options: The psychological and moral growth of the protagonist from youth to adulthood

questions.question: Assonance is the repetition of:
options: Consonant sounds at the start of words
Words at the end of lines

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of plot structure?
The term 'verisimilitude' in literature means:
options: The appearance of being true or real
A word that imitates a sound

questions.question: A dirge is a type of poem that:
Which of the following correctly defines 'point of view' in literature?
options: The perspective from which a story is narrated
The moral lesson of a story

questions.question: The term 'motif' in literature refers to:
options: A recurring element that has symbolic significance

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of modernist literature?
options: Stream of consciousness, fragmentation, and questioning of traditional values

questions.question: Pathetic fallacy is a technique where:
options: Weather or nature reflects a character's emotional state
A character blames others for their problems

questions.question: The epigraph of a literary work is:
options: A quotation at the beginning that hints at the theme

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of post-colonial African literature?
options: Exploration of identity, cultural conflict, and the effects of colonialism

questions.question: The term 'dénouement' refers to:
options: The rising action of a plot
The part of a plot where matters are explained or resolved
The introduction of the main character
The central conflict of the story

questions.question: Which of the following best describes an elegy?
options: A poem of serious reflection, typically mourning someone's death

questions.question: The unreliable narrator is one who:
options: Provides a limited or biased account that the reader cannot fully trust
Is the author of the story

questions.question: Intertextuality refers to:
options: The relationship between texts through allusions, quotations, or parallels

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the absurdist tradition in drama?
options: Emphasis on the meaninglessness of human existence and illogical situations

questions.question: The term 'archetype' in literature refers to:
options: The oldest manuscript of a text
A universally recognisable character type or symbol

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Petrarchan sonnet feature?
options: An octave (8 lines) and a sestet (6 lines)

questions.question: The concept of 'negative capability' was proposed by:
Which of the following is a characteristic of magical realism?
options: Blending of realistic narrative with fantastical or magical elements presented as normal

questions.question: The term 'hubris' in tragic literature refers to:
options: The tragic flaw of excessive pride that leads to a character's downfall
The comic relief in a serious play
A type of ancient Greek chorus
